messages.content: I'm refactoring the ingest pipeline. Currently it reads the whole folder into memory before dedup. I want to stream it. Worth it?

Depends on the sizes you expect. If corpora stay under ~2 GB, the in-memory version is simpler and fast enough. Over that, streaming matters — both for memory pressure and for letting the UI show progress earlier.
Target is user folders. So anywhere from 50 MB to 20 GB. Streaming then.

Agreed. Structure it as a pull iterator per source type (markdown, json chat, code) feeding a shared dedup stage. Keep the quality filter at the end; it's the expensive one and dedup kills most of its input.
Good. And the dedup — exact first, then shingle?
Yes. Exact is cheap and kills ~30% typically. Shingle MinHash on what remains. Keep both stats visible in the Dataset Doctor; users like seeing what got cut.
